sections.markdown:
- Activity: Afternoon Run
- Started: 2025-05-20 17:39
- Distance: 6.1 km
- Moving time: 32:38
- Elapsed time: 32:46
- Elevation gain: 110.4 m
- Average heart rate: 166.2 bpm
- Max heart rate: 178 bpm
- Average watts: 216.9 W
- Average speed: 11.2 km/h

- Apple Health recorded `4` during-run heart-rate samples. Average HR was `168.8 bpm`, ranging from `160.2` to `173.0 bpm`.
- First-half average HR was `165.3 bpm` and second-half average HR was `172.3 bpm` (+7.0 bpm), which shows a modest late rise in aerobic cost.

- Apple Health cadence averaged `173.7 spm`, with a working range of `164.0` to `210.0 spm` after excluding `1` start-stop outlier below `100 spm`.
- First-half cadence averaged `168.1 spm` and second-half cadence averaged `176.5 spm` (+8.4 spm), which suggests turnover lifted rather than falling away.

- Strava moving pace averaged `5:22 /km` across `6.077 km` in `32:38` moving time.
- Estimated first-half moving pace was `5:21 /km` and second-half pace was `5:24 /km` (+3 s/km), so pace stayed broadly even.

- Strava summary HR came in at `166.2 bpm` average and `178 bpm` max.
- Distance-split HR averaged `161.8 bpm` in the first half and `170.3 bpm` in the second (+8.5 bpm), which shows clear late cardiovascular drift.

- Moving time was `32:38` out of `32:46` elapsed (99.6%), which kept stoppage cost negligible.
- The downsampled moving stream shows `7` stop segments and `0.7%` stationary samples, so the run was mostly continuous with a few interruptions.

- Strava logged about `116.0 m` of climbing, with altitude moving between roughly `31.0 m` and `60.6 m`.
- Estimated climbing was `57.6 m` in the first half and `58.4 m` in the second, so the climbing load was split fairly evenly.
